title: Greenidge Historic Title V Air Permit - 5-Year Renewal with 44% Emissions Reduction
text: Greenidge and New York State Historic Air Permit Agreement. Title V Air Permit Terms: Five-year renewal of Title V Air Permit for Dresden NY facility. Mandated 44% reduction in permitted emissions by 2030. Exceeds ambitious goals of Climate Act (40% reduction). 25% reduction off facility actual emissions by 2030. Believed first such condition in Title V Air Permit renewal in New York State. Regulatory Settlement: Agreement signed by New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC). Greenidge to receive Final Title V Air Permit. New emissions levels fully consistent with New York Climate Leadership Community Protection Act. Dispute Resolution: Agreement formally ends all outstanding lawsuits and administrative appeals. Operations can proceed without interruption. Dramatic turnaround from 2022 initial air permit denial. Legal Victory: Late 2024 New York Supreme Court judge ruled in Greenidge favor. Settlement reflects resolution of lengthy legal dispute. Environmental Compliance: Operational requirements aligned with state climate goals. Dresden facility Dresden facility continues operating under renewed permit.
